message:
feat(store): OBJECT ENCODING listpack_ex/hashtable_ex + hexpired keyspace event (br-frankenredis-omff, rv89 part 4a)

Two upstream-facing surfaces for hash field TTL:

1. OBJECT ENCODING flip: when a hash carries at least one per-field
   TTL entry (any row in hash_field_expires), the encoding reply
   shifts from listpack/hashtable to listpack_ex/hashtable_ex, matching
   upstream 7.4. Uses a BTreeMap range probe on (key, _) so the check
   is O(log N) not O(N).

2. `hexpired` keyspace event on lazy reap: both drop_hash_field_if_expired
   and drop_expired_hash_fields now call notify_keyspace_event with
   NOTIFY_EXPIRED + "hexpired" + logical key for every field they
   reap. Convention matches the existing "expired" event for whole-key
   TTL expiry (lib.rs:1995).

2 new tests added (25 total in hash_field_ttl.rs):
- object_encoding_reports_listpack_ex_for_hash_with_any_field_ttl
- object_encoding_reports_hashtable_ex_when_big_hash_has_field_ttl

fr-store 280/0 lib tests + 25/0 hash_field_ttl + rest green; clippy clean.

Not yet landed (part 4a was scoped to encoding + lazy-reap keyspace
events only): command-side keyspace events on HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/
HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HPERSIST (need DB-context threading from
fr-command), DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields counter. Those go in a
follow-up alongside RDB (3jrv) and AOF propagation.

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>
